isolation.note: Dev server started on port 3102 (3100 and 3101 were in use). Neurosynth engine selected for testing.

steps.observed: App loaded with neurosynth and destrieux engines in sidebar

steps.expected: Predicate browser panel shows symbols grouped by category
steps.observed: Predicate browser loaded with Relations (4): PeakReported, Study, TermInStudyTFIDF, Voxel; Functions (32): agg_create_region, etc.; Probabilistic (1): SelectedStudy

steps.action: Type 'Peak' in the predicate browser search box
steps.expected: Predicate list filters to show only predicates matching 'Peak'
steps.observed: Predicate browser updated to show 'Relations 1' containing only 'PeakReported(0, 1, 2, 3)'. All other categories (Functions, Probabilistic) disappeared.

evidence.network: No additional network calls; filtering is client-side

assertions.title: Click predicate to add to query

steps.action: Click 'PeakReported(0, 1, 2, 3)' button in predicate browser (with 'Peak' search active)
steps.expected: PeakReported added to visual query builder as a predicate block with placeholder variables
steps.observed: Visual query builder updated to show 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)' with a remove button (x) on the PeakReported block. A 'Remove PeakReported from query' button also appeared.

evidence.network: No additional network calls for adding predicates

steps.action: Observe the visual query builder after adding PeakReported predicate
steps.expected: Visual representation shows a readable, structured rendering (not raw Datalog text) e.g. 'Find x, y, z where PeakReported(x, y, z, s)'
steps.observed: Visual query builder shows 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)' with keywords 'Find' and 'where' styled distinctly. The raw Datalog is shown separately below: 'Datalog: ans(v, v1, v2, v3) :- PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3).' — not as the primary visual representation.

frictions.description: The Vite dev server at http://localhost:3100 was not running. Ports 3100 and 3101 were both in use, so the dev server started on port 3102.
frictions.resolution: Manually started the dev server with 'npm run dev' from the neurolang-sparklis directory. Navigated to http://localhost:3102 instead.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. The predicate browser correctly displays symbols grouped by category after engine selection (VAL-QBUILD-001). Search/filter works correctly for substring matching (VAL-QBUILD-002). Clicking a predicate adds it to the visual query builder with placeholder variables (VAL-QBUILD-003). The visual query builder renders a readable natural-language-like form 'Find ... where ...' (VAL-QBUILD-004).
